description_long: This guard stone is sculpted in the form of a turtle supporting a tall, rectangular slab on its back. The turtle's body is robust, with four sturdy legs splayed outward and a detailed head extending from one end, giving the object a whimsical yet solid appearance. The slab is vertically oriented, smooth, and slightly weathered, suggesting it may serve as a marker or protective barrier. The entire object appears to be made from a single piece of stone, with a rough, matte finish and subtle variations in gray coloration. The turtle's shell and limbs are rounded and stylized, while the slab is geometric and functional. This object would be both decorative and utilitarian in an urban setting, likely intended to prevent vehicle encroachment or mark boundaries.
description: A gray stone guard stone shaped like a turtle carrying a tall, rectangular slab, with a rough, weathered surface and stylized features.
description_view_0: The front view shows the broad face of the rectangular slab and the turtle's two front legs, with the head partially visible at the base.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the turtle's head and extended neck, as well as the full length of the slab rising from the shell.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the back of the slab and the turtle's hind legs, with the shell's curvature visible.
description_view_3: The opposite side view shows the turtle's tail and the other side of the slab, with the head facing away.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the entrance to driveways, along the edge of sidewalks, or at the corners of historical sites to prevent vehicle intrusion and mark boundaries.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, standing upright with the turtle's feet resting on the surface and the slab vertical.
attribute_wear_patterns: Likely to show chipping and smoothing on the turtle's head, legs, and the lower edges of the slab due to occasional vehicle contact and foot traffic. The upper slab may display weathering and discoloration from prolonged exposure to the elements.
